name: Abalone Stuffed with Crabmeat
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a skillet and stir in flour. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
2. Cook, stirring, over medium heat about 1 minute but do not allow flour to brown.
3. Add milk and cook and stir 1 or 2 minutes until the mixture comes to boil and thickens. Set aside.
4. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a small saucepan. Add shallots and cook until tender but not browned.
5. Add crabmeat and heat thoroughly. Add enough white sauce to bind, about 1/2 cup. Season to taste with salt and white pepper and add red pepper, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, and lemon juice.
6. Carefully pound abalone steaks between 2 sheets of waxed paper until very thin (unless purchased already pounded). Dip abalone in eggs seasoned to taste with salt and white pepper. Coat with flour and set aside.
7. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a heavy skillet. Add abalone and brown quickly on one side. Turn and brown the other side. Do not overcook as abalone will toughen.
8. Place abalone steaks on a platter and spoon crabmeat stuffing onto each. Roll and arrange on serving platter, seam down. If desired, serve with any remaining sauce.
